## 1. Role Justification

The Lead Game Designer owns the game design vision from Stage 1 (Concept) through Stage 5 (Full Production). They author the GDD, define core game systems, economy design, and player retention strategies. This is a P0 hire because without a Lead Game Designer, the Creative Director cannot complete Stage 1 design deliverables, and no gameplay prototype (Stage 2) can be produced.

**Pipeline Stages Owned:** 1, 2, 3, 5

# Position Specification Document

**Role:** Lead Game Designer (G5)
**Role Family:** Creative / Design
**Seniority:** Principal (L3)
**Team:** Casual Games Studio — Creative/Design Division
**Reports To:** Creative Director (Sakura Ishimori)
**Priority:** P0
**Compensation Band:** $140K–$190K base
**Document ID:** PSD-2026-G5-001
**Generated:** 2026-04-10T08:00:00Z
**Generated By:** CHRO Office (Automated Pipeline)

---

## 1. Role Justification

The Lead Game Designer owns the game design vision from Stage 1 (Concept) through Stage 5 (Full Production). They author the GDD, define core game systems, economy design, and player retention strategies. This is a P0 hire because without a Lead Game Designer, the Creative Director cannot complete Stage 1 design deliverables, and no gameplay prototype (Stage 2) can be produced.

**Pipeline Stages Owned:** 1, 2, 3, 5

---

## 2. Required Competencies (weighted)

| Competency                          | Weight | Minimum Threshold | Assessment Method                        |
| ----------------------------------- | ------ | ----------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| Game Design Vision & GDD Authorship | 25%    | ≥ 4/5             | Portfolio review + GDD sample evaluation |
| Systems Design                      | 20%    | ≥ 4/5             | Systems design case study                |
| Economy Design & Monetization       | 15%    | ≥ 4/5             | Economy design challenge                 |
| Player Retention Strategies         | 15%    | ≥ 3/5             | Behavioral interview                     |
| Cross-Disciplinary Collaboration    | 10%    | ≥ 3/5             | Panel interview                          |
| Data-Informed Design Iteration      | 10%    | ≥ 3/5             | Case study                               |
| Design Team Leadership              | 5%     | ≥ 3/5             | Reference checks                         |

---

## 3. Must-Have Qualifications

- 8+ years game design experience
- GDD authorship for 2+ shipped titles
- Systems design expertise (progression loops, reward schedules, meta-game)
- Economy design experience (virtual currency, monetization, pricing)
- Player retention strategy track record (D1/D7/D30 metrics understanding)

---

## 4. Compensation Structure

| Component         | Range / Value                                      |
| ----------------- | -------------------------------------------------- |
| Base Salary       | $140K–$190K (positioned at 75th percentile: $165K) |
| Performance Bonus | Up to 15% of base                                  |
| Equity            | 0.15%–0.25% (Principal tier)                       |
| Signing Bonus     | Up to $25K (if leaving unvested equity)            |
| Relocation        | Up to $15K                                         |
| Conference Budget | $5K/year                                           |

---

## 5. Sourcing Channels (prioritized)

| Channel                         | Priority | Quality Weight |
| ------------------------------- | -------- | -------------- |
| King, Playrix, Scopely alumni   | 1        | 1.5×           |
| GDC Design Track speakers       | 2        | 1.3×           |
| Referrals from Sakura Ishimori  | 3        | 1.5×           |
| LinkedIn passive sourcing       | 4        | 1.0×           |
| Game design portfolio platforms | 5        | 1.1×           |

---

## 6. Assessment Battery

| Assessment                | Format                     | Duration | Weight |
| ------------------------- | -------------------------- | -------- | ------ |
| GDD Portfolio Review      | Automated rubric scoring   | Async    | 25%    |
| Systems Design Case Study | Core loop design challenge | 48h      | 25%    |
| Simulated Panel Interview | AI panel, signal questions | 45 min   | 30%    |
| Behavioral / Culture Add  | STAR method                | 30 min   | 15%    |
| Economy Design Challenge  | Virtual economy balancing  | 24h      | 5%     |

---

## 7. Success Criteria

| Milestone   | Criteria                                                                                              |
| ----------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Day 1**   | Design tools access granted, GDD template loaded, initial game concept brief reviewed                 |
| **Week 1**  | Core game loop documented; paired with Creative Director on Stage 1 GDD outline                       |
| **Month 1** | Complete GDD v1 drafted with systems, economy, and progression; reviewed by Creative Director         |
| **Month 3** | GDD approved; Stage 2 prototype design complete; design team (Senior Designer, Level Designer) ramped |

---
